ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Transfer Of Elm St. Property
Off Calendar Oct-02-2023 Continued To Dec-04-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard; PTR-20-303964 The Maureen Crowe trust dated July 16, 2016, and PTR-21-304179 Matter of the Lia Fail 2004 trust.

Appearances: Ross Madden appearing via zoom for and with Gregory Crowe appearing via zoom.
David Tate appeared in-person for Robert and Patricia Callahan.
James Fielders (trustee) appearing via zoom.
Leslie Anderson-Adams appeared via zoom as executor.

Mr. Tate confirms that the deed from Elm st. property has been recorded, that the property is ready to be listed for sale, that he is not sure what net proceeds are because Mr. Madden has not accounted for some monies, and that he would like to consolidate all three cases that are related including a civil matter. Mr. Madden agrees that all cases should be consolidated, that there is a $19,000.00 creditors claim by Mr. Crowe, and that going to trial on this matter should take 2 to 3 days. The Court orders a case management conference set for December 04 , 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. All matters are also continued to the same date.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Carbona: Not reported.

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jul-17-2023 Continued To Oct-02-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard; PTR-20-303964 The Maureen Crowe trust dated July 16, 2016 and PTR-21-304179 Matter of the Lia Fail 2004 trust.

Appearances; Ross Madden appearing via zoom for and with Gregory Crowe appearing via zoom.
David Tate appearing in-person for Robert and Patricia Callahan.
James Fielders (trustee) appearing via zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the sale of the elm street property, that the property is being mismanaged, that the property is only worth around $110,000.00, and that the proceeds from the sale of the property should be placed into a blocked account. The Court orders the removal of James Fielders as the trustee of the Maureen Crowe 2016 trust and remove Gregory Crowe as trustee of Lia Fail 2004 trust and appoint Leslie O. Anderson as the successor trustee of both trusts. The Court orders Mr. Tate to submit a formal order. Status of proof of insurance for Elm Street, the Petition for Removal, Appointment or Confirmation of Trustee. Etc., the Accounting Petition, the Petition to Determine Distribution of Trust Property and are continued to October 02, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of transfer of elm st. property is also set for October 02, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Singh: Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Continued To Jul-17-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard; PTR-20-303964 The matter of the Maureen E. Crowe Trust dated July 16, 2016 and PTR-21-304179 Matter of Lia Fail 2004 Trust.
Appearances; Ross Madden appearing via zoom for and with Gregory Crowe appearing via zoom.
David Tate appearing in-person for Robert and Patricia Callahan.
James Fielders (trustee) appearing via zoom.

Mr. Tate stated that there are two orders that they would like to present to the Court, that one has to do with Elm St. property for Mr. Preston to record the 2016 deed so he can sell it, and that the other order has to do with consolidating the other three cases and vacating the June 5th trial date. The Court stated that it is not prepared to sign the second order, that it cannot vacate a trial date for department 206, that it generally will not consolidate civil cases with trust matters and instructed counsel to do an ex parte drop-off petition with respect to the Elm St. property. The Court also instructed Mr. Madden to deliver a courtesy copy of the February 2, 2023 accounting petition along with a proposed order. Further discussion was held regarding Mr. Crowes health issues and his inability to provide information, Mr. Maddens multiple efforts to get information from Mr. Crowe, the possibility of Mr. Crowe giving Mr. Madden authority to gain access to the financial records Mr. Tate not having issued subpoenas to avoid additional expense to his client, the issue of how much money went to the attorneys, the note from the healthcare provider being generic, the bank not being able to provide information with respect to where the wire transfer went, Mr. Madden possibly being able to do a simple accounting but not having information to know meaningful events, the issue of showing who owned Hodges Alley, the issue of conflict of interest with Mr. Tate's representation of Patricia Callahan and the executor and beneficiary, etc. The Court stated that it would like to see the Elm Street property to be distributed, that its orders stand, and that it wants Mr. Madden and Mr. Crowe to make efforts to get things done not-withstanding Mr, Crowes illness. Status of mediation is off calendar. Status of proof of insurance for Elm Street, the Petition for Removal, Appointment or Confirmation of Trustee. Etc., the Accounting Petition and the Petition to Determine Distribution of Trust Property are continued to July 17, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge; Ross Moody; Clerk: Ateesh Datt; Deputy: Choi; Not reported.

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Mar-01-2023 Continued To May-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-20-303964 The Maureen E. Crowe Trust and
PTR-21-304179 The Lia Fail 2004 Trust.

Appearances: David Tate appeared by zoom for petitioners.
Ross Madden appeared by zoom for and with Gregory Crowe by zoom.
James Feilders (trustee) appeared by zoom in propria persona.

The court noted that a significant accounting was prepared. Mr. Tate stated that he will be doing a formal objection to the accounting, that 75% percent of the proceeds for Hodges Valley have not been deposited into a blocked account, that the accounting is incomplete and does not contain documentation indicating where the money went, and that the Elm Street property can not be insured because it is resided in. Mr. Tate also stated that there is $180,000.00 that has gone to the attorneys, but they don't know what attorneys and for what client, that Elm Street needs to be fixed up in order to rent it, or has to be sold, and that everyone expects it to be sold at a loss and that the loss be split. Mr. Madden explained that the accounting is not finished, that it is not balanced, that he thinks it will be resolved once he gets information from his client, and that there are around 1500 pages of statements. Mr. Madden also stated that he followed what the court said and filed the accounting, that he needs more time to finish it, that he doesn't think the money exist, that he believes the money was distributed before this litigation commenced, and that his client has no interest in Elm Street. Mr. Feilders stated that he recorded the deed, ask to be allowed to distribute the property, and stated that he is confident that the loss will be very minimal. Mr. Tate stated that $800,000.00 to $900,000.00 was generated from the sale of Hodges, that the money went to Mr. Crowe and was paid to attorneys' fees, that Mr. Crowe has to account for the money that he distributed to himself a account for what went to the attorneys. The court encourages the parties to meet and confer, and stated that they can do a drop off ex parte petition regarding the deed. The hearing date on the accounting of April 18, 2023 is continued to May 10, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of mediation (2), status of proof of insurance for Elm Street, the Petition for Removal, Appointment or Confirmation of Trustee. Etc., and the Petition to Determine Distribution of Trust Property are also continued to May 10 , 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Nov-14-2022 Continued To Feb-01-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross Madden, via Zoom, for and with Gregory Crowe, Respondent; David Tate, via Zoom, for Patricia and Robert Callahan, Petitioners and Leslie Anderson Adams, via Zoom, Personal Representative of related Estate matter. James Feilders, via Zoom, in propria persona, Respondent.

The court confirms with Mr. Madden that the proceeds from the sale of the Hodges Alley property is between $700,000-$800,000. The court instructs Mr. Madden that 3/4 of the net proceeds from the sale must be deposited and he must file an ex parte application for a court order to deposit those funds in a blocked account and to file the closing statement. Mr. Crowe asks that the court reconsider its position regarding the blocked account and offers advocacy for his request. Messrs. Madden and Tate presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the court's order regarding the deposit of proceeds in the blocked account. Mr. Madden replies that he cannot confirm the amount that remains from the sale of the proceeds from the sale of the Hodges Alley property but believes it is fair that the court request the deposit of 3/4 and then provide an accounting. The court advises Mr. Madden that his client must pay outstanding expenses related to the Elm St. property and confirm that the property is insured. Mr. Tate reports that there is an agreement to sell the Elm St. property as it cannot be sold because it is underwater. Mr. Tate suggests appointing Leslie Anderson-Adams as successor trustee, to handle the sale of the Elm St. property. Mr. Madden agrees that the Elm St. property should be sold but that it should be done via the Estate matter.

After additional discussion, the court rescinds its order regarding Mr. Crowe to pay outstanding expenses related to the Elm St. property and confirm that the property is insured. The matters are continued to Feb-01-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Sep-12-2022 Continued To Nov-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Tate, via Zoom, for Robert and Patricia Callahan, Petitioners/Objectors; Ross Madden, via Zoom, for and with Gregory Crowe, Petitioner/Objector; James Feilders, via Zoom, in propria persona, Trustee.

The court mentions the current status of this case especially regarding the pending motion in the related Fail Trust matter, PTR-21-304179. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Trust matters, the failure to properly insure the property located on Elm Street, and of the existence of a deed from the year 2016, signed by Decedent Maureen Crowe. Mr. Crowe reports on his efforts with the trust and of the issues since Leslie Anderson Adams' appointment as Personal Representative in the related Estate matter. Mr. Tate requests that the court order Mr. Crowe to account. Mr. Ross objects to Mr. Crowe's request. The court advises that it will not take action on Mr. Tate's request, but his request for an accounting is noted. The court confirms that the summary motion set in the related Fail Trust matter will proceed as scheduled. The court orders Mr. Feilders to cooperate and sign any documents to ensure that the Elm Street Property is properly insured.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Nov-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Apr-11-2022 Continued To Jul-11-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Tate, via Zoom, for Robert and Patricia Callahan, Petitioners; James Feilders, via Zoom, in propria persona, Trustee; Ross Madden, via Zoom, for Gregory Crowe, Objector.

The court queries Mr. Feilders regarding why he did not appear at the deposition. Mr. Feilders reports that he did not appear because he believed that the parties were close to resolving the matter via mediation. Mr. Madded reports that the parties are not that far apart and might be able to close the gap and reach a resolution with another mediation session. Mr. Tate offers oral testimony refuting both Mr. Feilders and Mr. Madden's testimony and points the court to page three (3) of his Status Conference Statement. The court advises Mr. Feilders that he will be removed as Trustee if he does not participate in any future depositions. The court confirms with Messrs. Tate and Madden that they intend to file individual Motions for Summary Judgment.

After additional discussion, the matters are continued to Jul-11-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Mr. Feilders is ordered to attend any depositions by Jun-10-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Sep-27-2021 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Sep-27-2021 Continued To Dec-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross Madden, via ZOOM, for Gregory Crowe, Petitioner; David Tate, via ZOOM, for Patricia Callahan and Robert Callahan, Petitioners in related action (PTR-20-303964); Amy Martinez, via ZOOM, for James Feilders, Respondent in related action (PTR-20-303964).

Mr. Madden begins by advising the court that the underlying issue in this matter and related matter is the purported deeds that were delivered and/or accepted and that this issue should be set for trial. Mr. Tate agrees that the matters should be set for trial but that the issues that should be adjudicated are more than those that Mr. Madden has reported. Mr. Tate then provides oral support for his position and believes that these matters may be resolved quicker if the court appoints Leslie Anderson-Adams as Successor Trustee and provides oral testimony in support of his suggestion. Mr. Madden then provides oral testimony in support of his position regarding this matter and related matter. Ms. Martinez advises the court that she believes that Mr. Feilders should not be removed as Trustee. Mr. Madden and Mr. Tate provide additional oral testimony in support of their positions. The court then encourages counsel to work together and advises the parties that it will be ordering them to mediation.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Nov-29-2021. The Parties are to notify the assigned Probate Examiner of the status of mediation by Dec-06-2021 and statements regarding the status of mediation must be filed by Dec-06-2021. The Parties are to return on Dec-13-2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Petition to Determine, etc., is continued to Dec-13-2021 at 9:00 am, in Department 204. The Case Management Hearing is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: E. Prado. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Distribution Of Trust Property
Off Calendar Jul-19-2021 Continued To Sep-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: David Tate, via ZOOM, for Patricia Callahan and David Callahan, Petitioners; Amy Martinez, via ZOOM, for James Feilders, Respondent; Ross Madden, via ZOOM, for Gregory Crowe, Respondent.

The court begins by querying counsel as to the status of this matter and whether the parties may be willing to participate in pro bono mediation as it appears to be applicable in this case. Mr. Madden and Ms. Martinez believe that this matter could benefit from mediation. Mr. Tate reports that he and his client are amenable to mediation but not until Mr. Crowe deposits funds into a blocked account. Mr. Madden also suggests this matter and the related trust matter be consolidated. The court advises the parties that it will be setting a Case Management hearing for the next calling of this case.

The Petition to Determine Distribution of Trust Property is continued to September 27, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Case Management hearing is set for the same date. A Joint Case Management Statement is to be filed with the court by no later than September 13, 2021.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: B. Quesada. Not reported.
